Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Paul D. Smith of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0339), appearing for intervenor and moving party American States Insurance Company.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Mary Beth Perniz.

RULING: INTERVENOR AMERICAN STATES INSURANCE COMPANY NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO DISMISS FOR INSUFFICIENCY OF SERVICE OF PROCESS
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenor American States Insurance Company's Motion to Dismiss for Insufficiency of Service of Process is denied. Plaintiff substantially complied with CCP 416.10(a) by serving Aladdin's designated agent for service of process via substitute service. (Dill v. Berquist Constr. Co. (1994) 24 Cal. App. 4th 1426, 1436-37; see also Trackman v. Kenney (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 175, 185 ["Leaving papers with an apparent co-resident at an address publicly registered by the defendant, as required by law, is a method of service reasonably calculated to achieve actual service, and is therefore facially valid, whether or not actual service is accomplished on the facts of a given case"].) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Mary J. Visciglio of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10391, mjvisciglio@sbcglobal.net (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

RULING: INTERVENOR AMERICAN STATES INSURANCE COMPANY (A SAFECO COMPANY)'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR INSUFFICIENT SERVICE OF PROCESS
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Intervenor American States Insurance Company (A Safeco Company)'s Motion to Dismiss for Insufficient Service of Process is denied without prejudice. Intervenor lacks standing to bring this motion as it failed to file an answer-in-intervention within 30 days of the April 3, 2019 hearing on its Motion to Set Aside Default and Seek Leave to File an Answer-In-Intervention, as stated in the Court's order. The Court did not consider Intervenor's moving papers or reply papers for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) Order signed in open court. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Hearing Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for intervenor / moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company on behalf of its suspended insured, defendant Associated Insulation of California, Inc.
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Beth Perniz as successor in interest to and as wrongful death heir of John L. Perniz, deceased, et al.

RULING:
INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY ON BEHALF OF ITS SUSPENDED INSURED, DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenor Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's (on behalf of Associated Insulation of California, Inc.) motion for summary judgment is denied. Assuming Intervenor sustained its initial burden, the deposition testimony and declaration of Robert Mingram create a triable issue whether decedent John Perniz was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (pipe insulation) attributable to Associated Insulation. Intervenor fails to demonstrate that the arguments it makes regarding Associated not being negligent and not supplying or manufacturing any products at decedent's jobsites warrant summary judgment. Plaintiffs' objections to excerpts of Barry Castleman's book (Exhibit J) and Charles Ay's deposition testimony (Exhibit K) are sustained. Intervenor's objections to Robert Mingram's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Nicole Hatler of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13730 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
